pattern: Since more digits always beats fewer (digits are only 1 or 2, never a leading zero to worry about), the best you can ever do is keep the string as long as possible while still ending on a 2 -- and that forces every character before the last surviving 2 to stay, so the answer is simply everything up to and including the final 2 in the string.
hints: A longer kept string is always numerically bigger than a shorter one here, since every character is either 1 or 2 and there's no leading-zero concern -- so maximize length first.
Whatever you keep has to end on a '2' for the result to be even. To keep it as long as possible, that final '2' should be the very last one that appears anywhere in the string.

Once you've picked which '2' will be the last character you keep, every character before it must also be kept (dropping any of them only shortens the result for no benefit) -- so the answer is just the string truncated right after its last '2', or empty if there is no '2' at all.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, string already ends in the largest possible even value untouched
statement example, trailing digit must be dropped to reach an even ending
statement example, no 2 anywhere, impossible to make even
single character that is a 2, shortest possible even answer
all ones except the single last character, must keep everything before it too
a 2 appears early with several 1s trailing after it, that early 2 cannot be used since keeping it would drop the later, larger prefix
several trailing ones after the last 2 must all be dropped

stress.description: random strings of 1s and 2s up to the constraint's max length
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100))
    length = rng.randint(1, n)
    s = ''.join(rng.choice('12') for _ in range(length))
    return [s]
